title: Is every 7-regular Ramanujan graph globally rigid in R^2?
publication_narrative_strength: good if the remaining residue closes; current slice alone is too thin
micro_paper_assessment: A real theorem-facing separator slice was obtained, but the current package is still too thin for the micro-paper lane because 4-cut templates for n=16,18 and 5-cut templates generally remain alive.
generalization_signal: moderate; separator-plus-interlacing scales to tiny cuts but weakens at larger separators
candidate_theorem_slice: Every 7-regular Ramanujan graph on 16, 18, or 20 vertices has no vertex cut of size at most 3; moreover, a 20-vertex 7-regular Ramanujan graph has no vertex cut of size 4.
next_action: Tighten the surviving 4-cut templates for n=16 and n=18, then attack the s=5 residue using additional rigidity structure or local residue tables rather than more raw quotient bounds.
